_meta.purpose: Frozen model comparison artifact for portfolio review. Every number traces to a saved training run.
_meta.dataset: DataCo Smart Supply Chain — 180,519 order line items / 65,752 orders
_meta.primary_metric: F1 (55/45 class split makes accuracy misleading)
_meta.regen_note: 2026-07-11: corrected temporal XGBoost confusion matrix; added threshold sweep at 0.05 / 0.35 / 0.5; fixed train/test sizes to order-level.

temporal.note: Headline split — orders sorted by date, train on past / test on future.

random.note: Stratified random split used only to confirm temporal stability.

xgboost_default_random.note: Close to temporal F1 (0.6648) — confirms no temporal degradation.

calibration.note: Poorly calibrated out of the box. CalibratedModelAdapter (isotonic/Platt) exists and is unit-tested, but is not wired into train.py or the Streamlit serving path.

operating_point.rationale: A pure expected-cost sweep under FN=3×FP picks threshold 0.05 (recall 1.0, flag_rate 1.0) — flag everything. That minimizes the toy cost function but is operationally useless. Threshold 0.35 is an ops compromise: raises recall from 0.5825 → 0.8013 while keeping precision at 0.6145 and flag_rate ≈ 0.72.

at_0_05.note: Pure cost-opt under FN=3×FP; not used in the demo.

headline_finding: XGBoost adds no meaningful lift over logistic regression (F1 0.6648 vs 0.6531). Both models concentrate importance on shipping_mode (~73% of mean |SHAP|). The recommended production answer is a shipping-mode rule plus a model only for the ambiguous Standard Class tier. The demo currently scores all modes with the uncalibrated model at threshold 0.35. See docs/LIMITATIONS_AND_DECISIONS.md.
